title: Barefoot Champion Races to Olympic Glory
story: Abebe Bikila of Ethiopia sprinted into history on October 21, 1964. At the Tokyo Olympics, he blazed through the marathon in a world record time. Bikila finished in just over 2 hours and 12 minutes, leaving his closest rival more than 4 minutes behind. This incredible feat made him the first athlete ever to win the Olympic marathon twice!
activity_question: What country was Abebe Bikila from?
bonus_fact: Abebe Bikila famously ran barefoot when he won his first Olympic marathon in 1960.

title: Old Ironsides Sets Sail
story: Cannons boomed as a mighty warship slid into Boston Harbor. The USS Constitution, nicknamed 'Old Ironsides', began her journey in 1797 on October 21. This wooden frigate was one of the first ships built for the young U.S. Navy. Her sturdy oak hull earned her famous nickname when enemy cannonballs seemed to bounce right off! Today, she's the oldest commissioned warship still afloat.

title: Magellan's Daring Ocean Voyage
story: Brave explorer Ferdinand Magellan led his fleet on an incredible journey in 1520. After battling fierce winds and treacherous waters, they finally reached Cape Virgenes on October 21. Their groundbreaking voyage opened up new paths for future explorers.
